title: Water Inlet Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher is not receiving enough water.
fullDescription: Error i10 indicates insufficient water supply. Check the water tap, inlet hose, and filter screens.

causes.cause: Primary component related to water inlet error has failed

symptoms: Issue related to water inlet error

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug for 5 minutes. Restart and test.

fixSteps.title: Clean filters and check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the drain filter, inlet filter, and check all accessible connections.

fixSteps.title: Test relevant component
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the component indicated by the error code.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty part
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the identified faulty component and run a test cycle.

whenToCallPro: If basic troubleshooting does not resolve the error, contact an AEG service technician.

faq.q: What does AEG dishwasher error i10 mean?
faq.a: The dishwasher is not receiving enough water.
Many AEG dishwasher errors can be resolved with basic troubleshooting like cleaning filters, checking hoses, and resetting the machine.
If your dishwasher is within the AEG warranty period, contact AEG service for a free repair.

fullDescription: Error i20 means the drain pump could not empty the sump. Check drain filter, hose, and pump.
